payload.goal: Build the Harness v1 engine: event log core, MCP server, hooks, projections, CLI, watcher, dialect — engine only, schema swappable

plan.goal: Build the Harness v1 engine during the Fable 5 window (Jul 3–7): event log core, MCP server, Claude Code hooks, projections, CLI, watcher/state server, and the AGENTS.md dialect. Engine only — schema lives in one swappable module; UI/sync/team are explicitly out of scope. Full contracts in docs/SPEC.md; conventions in CLAUDE.md; strategy context in harness-docs/ (user-held, outside this repo).

tasks.title: Scaffold: TS strict, vitest, esbuild bundling, npm bin entry
Event envelope types + runtime validation (SPEC §Envelope)
Append: atomic single-line O_APPEND writes, concurrent-safe
Fold/replay: events.jsonl → InitiativeState (SPEC §State)
Cursor primitive: export/import "events since N" (sync-ready)
Tests: concurrent appends, corrupt-line tolerance (skip+warn), replay determinism, cursor round-trip
stdio MCP server exposing typed tools (SPEC §MCP tools)
Tools: get_state, start_session, end_session, update_task, log_decision, update_plan, add_note
Payload schemas isolated in packages/schema/ (the ONLY schema home) — satisfied by construction
.mcp.json registration snippet emitted by init (Phase 4 wires it)
Tests: every tool appends correct event; state reflects it
Hook shims in .claude/hooks/ as standalone scripts calling the CLI (portability rule)
SessionStart shim: emit projection as context, ≤10,000 chars
PostToolUse shim (Edit|Write|MultiEdit|Bash): append file_touched / command_run
Stop shim: no session_ended for this session → exit 2 "write back"; stop_hook_active guard
SessionEnd shim: mechanical close event (fallback logging only)
Projection generator: templates → plan.md, decisions.md, status block; regenerated on every append; never hand-edited
harness init: scaffold .harness/, install hook shims + settings, emit .mcp.json entry, CLAUDE.md protocol block asserting total jurisdiction (BD19)
harness new <slug> / harness switch <slug>: initiative dirs + bindings.json (branch ↔ initiative)
harness status: fold + print tree (phase/task/status/next)
harness export --since <cursor> / harness import
Watcher + localhost JSON state server (no UI — endpoint only)
AGENTS.md protocol block (convention dialect for MCP-less tools)
OpenCode adapter notes: plugin equivalents documented; convention fallback verified manually (docs + simulated checklist done; manual OpenCode run pending, BD32)
THE CEREMONY: final Fable write-back via protocol; Opus 4.8 resumes from the record alone; score as arm-C on the Phase 0 scorecard (execution done; user-held scoring remains)
README.md: what/why, install, quickstart, MCP server, hooks, AGENTS.md dialect — npm front door
Packaging: bundled-bin tarball installs with zero runtime deps; npm pack → install → init E2E
Atomic projection writes (temp file + rename)
CLI version single-sourced from package.json
repo.md surfaces in the SessionStart context with its own budget; ≤10,000-char cap holds
Adopt-by-id sessions: context carries session id; harness_start_session adopts ONLY on exact id match; newest-open heuristic removed (BD43)
Derived resume fallback: fold aggregates per-session activity; surfaced when a session lacks a written summary (BD44)
Acceptance: two interleaved sessions on ONE initiative — correct attribution, independent Stop gates, no cross-adoption; unwritten session yields usable resume block

phases.name: Phase 8 — Uninstall + adoption of existing records

tasks.title: harness uninit [--purge]: strip exactly what init installed, preserve user content; record kept by default; byte-clean fresh-repo round-trip
harness adopt <legacy-file> [slug] [--mark]: env checks, replay brief with dialect commands + retirement checklist, idempotent superseded banner; NO markdown parsing
Acceptance: round-trip hashes; uninit preserves foreign content; adopt brief executed as scripted commands ends with status reflecting the legacy plan

payload.chose: BD1: Stack = TypeScript/Node ≥18 across CLI/MCP/shims, distributed via npm
payload.over: Rust/Go (slower to build in the window, MCP SDK maturity); Python (weak global-CLI distribution story)
payload.because: MCP SDK is TS-first; users already have Node (Claude Code requires it); npm is the distribution channel; one language across CLI/MCP/shims

payload.chose: BD2: Write-back enforcement = Stop hook (exit 2 blocks stop, forces continue), with stop_hook_active check guarding against infinite loops
payload.over: SessionEnd hook
payload.because: SessionEnd is cleanup-only and cannot feed back to the agent; Stop can block and force the write-back

payload.chose: BD3: Status projection injected at SessionStart is summary-dense; detail lives in per-session files
payload.over: (no alternative recorded)
payload.because: the projection must fit the 10,000-char context cap

payload.chose: BD4: Hooks are thin standalone scripts calling `harness event append` — logic lives in the CLI
payload.over: command logic inline in the hook scripts
payload.because: shims stay portable across Claude Code/Codex when they carry no logic

payload.chose: BD5: Events are truth; md files are generated projections — hand-edits to projections are a bug
payload.over: (no alternative recorded)
payload.because: per the 03-architecture storage decision

payload.chose: BD6: Schema (event payloads + projection templates) confined to src/schema/ and src/projections/templates/ (paths superseded by BD11: packages/schema/src/ and packages/engine/src/projections/templates/)
payload.over: (no alternative recorded)
payload.because: Phase 0/1 schema iteration touches only these locations

payload.chose: BD7: devDependencies typescript, esbuild, vitest, @types/node allowed as toolchain; runtime dependency set stays exactly @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, commander, chokidar, ulid
payload.over: (no alternative recorded)
payload.because: toolchain is not runtime deps — the CLAUDE.md dependency rule itself names vitest/esbuild as the test/build tools

payload.chose: BD8: Correction fold semantics — a correction event VOIDS the event its ref points at (target skipped during replay); replacement content is appended as a fresh event
payload.over: in-place patching
payload.because: patching violates event immutability and complicates replay; SPEC only says "referencing the target id", so voiding is the minimal meaningful interpretation

payload.chose: BD9: current.* fields are DERIVED at fold end, not stored — active_phase = first active phase; next_action = latest session_ended's next_action; blocked_on = blocked phases/tasks (blocking note when present, cleared on unblock); envelope-valid events of unknown type still advance the cursor
payload.over: storing redundant state-sync events in the log
payload.because: SPEC leaves the computation unspecified; deriving keeps the log free of redundant state; sync moves events by envelope, not payload

payload.chose: BD10: ulid generation uses monotonicFactory(), not the default ulid()
payload.over: default ulid() — randomly ordered within the same millisecond
payload.because: random same-ms ordering breaks the cursor contract (creation order must match sort order for "events since id"); cross-process same-ms ordering stays unspecified, acceptable because fold replays in file order

payload.chose: BD11: Monorepo now (user-directed, Jul 3) — npm workspaces, zero new tooling: packages/schema = @harness/schema (source-shipped internal package, no build step until it publishes); packages/engine = harness bin; SPEC §Repo layout + CLAUDE.md guard-rail paths updated; BD6 path references superseded
payload.over: deferring to v2 (the prior recommendation); pnpm/turborepo
payload.because: user prefers paying the structure cost early so ui/sync/adapters slot in later; npm workspaces add no dependencies

payload.chose: BD12: MCP server uses the SDK's LOW-LEVEL API (Server + setRequestHandler(ListTools/CallTool)) with hand-written plain-JSON-Schema tool definitions and @harness/schema validators (packages/schema/src/tool-inputs.ts)
payload.over: the high-level McpServer.registerTool API
payload.because: registerTool wants zod shapes and zod is not in the locked runtime dependency set (BD7); hand-rolled validators keep every validation shape in the one schema home (guard-rail)

payload.chose: BD13: MCP server launches via `harness mcp [--root <dir>]` on the existing commander CLI; SPEC §CLI extended one line; .mcp.json snippet {mcpServers:{harness:{command:"harness",args:["mcp"]}}} emitted by src/mcp/register.ts, wired into `harness init` in Phase 4
payload.over: a separate bin entry (harness-mcp); improvising an undocumented surface without extending SPEC
payload.because: one bin keeps the install/registration surface minimal; SPEC did not list the command, so a one-line SPEC extension beats improvisation

payload.chose: BD14: Projections seam built in Phase 2 — regenerateProjections(initiativeDir, state) renders minimal v0 plan.md + decisions.md via template functions; every tool append calls it; generated files carry a "do not hand-edit" header; Phase 3 extends the templates in place
payload.over: deferring projections entirely to Phase 3; building full templates in Phase 2
payload.because: deferral would violate the SPEC tool contract (every tool regenerates projections) and Phase 2 acceptance; full templates now would be Phase 3 scope creep

payload.chose: BD15: Session semantics — the stdio server process holds ONE in-memory active session: start_session mints a ulid, appends session_started, sets it active; subsequent appends use the active id/tool with actor "agent"; end_session's session_id arg wins over the active session and the slot clears after the append
payload.over: a multi-session registry
payload.because: one agent process per stdio server — YAGNI

payload.chose: BD16: Initiative resolution — explicit `initiative` arg wins; else current git branch → slug via .harness/bindings.json, branch read by parsing .git/HEAD (following worktree-style gitdir files); detached HEAD/no repo/no binding → typed unknown_initiative error; resolved slugs must exist under .harness/initiatives/ (typos must not create logs); repo root = server cwd, `harness mcp --root` overrides
payload.over: spawning `git rev-parse`
payload.because: a subprocess per call, needs git on PATH, and HEAD parsing is sufficient for v1

payload.chose: BD17: Typed tool errors — failures return isError:true with content[0].text = JSON {code, message, errors?}; code union lives in ONE place (@harness/schema/tool-inputs): invalid_input | unknown_initiative | unknown_tool | unknown_event | io_error; validation failures carry field-level errors; no event is appended on failure
payload.over: MCP protocol errors (McpError)
payload.because: agents see tool-result text, not protocol faults — in-band typed errors are actionable and parseable

payload.chose: BD18: Tool arg → payload mapping at the tool boundary — update_task {task_id, status, note?} → task_status_changed {id, status, note?}; update_plan {plan} validated by the existing PlanStructure validator via validatePayload; log_decision → decision_logged; add_note → note_added
payload.over: changing either frozen contract (SPEC tool signature says task_id, Phase 1 payload schema says id)
payload.because: mapping at the boundary keeps both contracts frozen; reusing validatePayload means tool input and event payload cannot drift

payload.chose: BD19: Total-jurisdiction protocol block (user field finding, Jul 4) — SPEC §CLI init contract expanded: the installed block MUST state (a) all work state lives in harness records, never tool memory or scratch files; (b) work matching no existing initiative requires `harness new` first; (c) bindings resolve which record a session serves. Affects tasks 4.1 and 5.1. The same hand-edit reverted SPEC §Repo layout/§Event types to pre-monorepo text and dropped the `harness mcp` line — restored
payload.over: per-file jurisdiction only
payload.because: dogfooding showed a second initiative's state leaking into Claude Code native memory + a scratch dir — per-file jurisdiction is not enough

payload.chose: BD20: Session identity correlation through the log, no side channel — the hook appends session_started with envelope.session = Claude's session_id; harness_start_session ADOPTS the newest open session for the initiative (no duplicate append), minting a fresh ulid only when none is open; the Stop shim verifies write-back by folding the log for Claude's session_id (adoption heuristic later REMOVED by BD43)
payload.over: a side-channel correlation file (second source of truth beside the log); passing session ids through env vars
payload.because: the log is the one source of truth; env vars are not available to the MCP server process

payload.chose: BD21: New event type session_closed {reason} — the mechanical close from the SessionEnd hook: fold sets session.ended only (never summary/next_action, never overrides an earlier end), warns + skips unregistered sessions (no stubs); SPEC §Event types updated; SPEC §State sessions gained model?/next_action?
payload.over: reusing session_ended with a placeholder summary
payload.because: a fabricated summary would clobber the fold-derived current.next_action and defeat the Stop shim's write-back check

payload.chose: BD22: Best-effort hooks — every `harness event` handler exits 0 silently on any failure; only Stop deliberately exits 2, and only for a registered-but-unwritten session (stop_hook_active → 0 loop guard; unregistered session_id → 0 so foreign repos are never blocked; write-back check = fold-derived session.summary present); SessionStart re-fires skip the duplicate append but still print the status block; handlers are pure functions returning {exitCode, stdout, stderr}
payload.over: erroring loudly
payload.because: a memory layer that can break sessions in unbound repos is worse than no memory layer; thin commander wiring keeps tests free of process.exit

payload.chose: BD23: PostToolUse mapping — Edit|MultiEdit → file_touched {path, op:"edit"}, Write → op:"write", Bash → command_run {cmd}; envelope source "hook", actor "agent", session = stdin session_id (fallback "cli"); unknown tool_name or missing file_path/command → exit 0, append nothing
payload.over: recording tool_response; an op per hook tool name
payload.because: tool_response is verbose with no state value; the record cares about mutation kind (edit|write), not which editor variant did it

payload.chose: BD24: Status projection ≤10k strategy — fixed per-section char budgets (goal 600, next_action/blocked 500, session summary 1200, 5 decisions × 280, phase list ≤12 lines; worst case ~6.5k) plus a final enforceStatusLimit slice to 10,000 with a truncation notice; the status block is stdout-only (no status.md file); sessions/<id>.md regenerated for every known session on every append with filenames sanitized ([^A-Za-z0-9._-] → _)
payload.over: proportional (single-pool) budgeting
payload.because: fixed per-section caps are easier to reason about for worst cases; session ids come from outside, so hostile ids must stay inside sessions/

payload.chose: BD25: `harness init` mechanics — shim sources ship INSIDE the bundled CLI (esbuild sh-as-text loader; vitest mirrors it); byte-level idempotency via write-if-changed; ownership rules: shims harness-owned (kept current, 0755), repo.md hand-written (create-only), CLAUDE.md protocol block installs once between <!-- harness:protocol --> markers and is never touched again (hand edits inside survive); settings.json/.mcp.json merged with existing entries respected; unparseable user JSON aborts init exit 1
payload.over: reading shims from the package dir; JSON-format-preserving edits
payload.because: package-dir reads break packaging (only dist/ ships); idempotency only needs OUR formatting to be stable; merging must never risk a clobber

payload.chose: BD26: `harness new`/`switch` semantics — CLI-created events carry actor "human", source "cli", session "cli"; new resolves the branch BEFORE creating anything so a bind failure leaves the repo untouched; --no-bind is the detached-HEAD/no-repo escape hatch; omitted --goal → non-empty placeholder; bindings.json writes are read-modify-write merges; switch refuses unknown slugs (typos must not create logs — BD16 extended to the CLI)
payload.over: binding "worktree name" instead of branch
payload.because: SPEC §Record layout keys bindings by branch-or-worktree and currentBranch() already resolves worktree HEADs to their branch

payload.chose: BD27: `harness status` render lives in src/projections/templates/status.ts as renderFullStatus beside the capped renderStatus — same fold, uncapped, per-task tree ([x]/[~]/[!]/[ ]); fold warnings → stderr with exit 0; resolution failures exit 1 with the BD16 typed message + usage hint
payload.over: reusing the capped renderStatus for the CLI; putting the render in src/cli/
payload.because: the 10k cap is a SessionStart context budget (BD3), not a terminal constraint — caps would silently hide tasks; templates are the only template home (guard-rail); a corrupt line must not hide status

payload.chose: BD28: export/import both take an optional [slug] positional resolving like status (explicit wins, else branch binding, BD16); SPEC §CLI extended to match; import summary is one parseable JSON line {"appended":N,"skipped":M}; stream warnings → stderr; projections regenerate only when appended > 0; handlers take the already-read stream
payload.over: an export command that can only serve the bound initiative
payload.because: the BD19 field finding showed moving a second initiative's log is a real workflow; an idempotent re-import rewrites nothing; commander owns file/stdin IO so tests stay process-free

payload.chose: BD29: `harness serve` — node:http only, bound 127.0.0.1 ONLY, three routes (/state, /state/<slug>, /events SSE; non-GET 405, else 404, slugs validated [a-z0-9-]+); chokidar watches events.jsonl by basename, re-folds just the changed initiative, pushes state (measured 51ms vs ≤500ms contract); startServer factory awaits watcher-ready, close() releases everything; 15s heartbeat; a fold error degrades to empty state for that slug, not a 500; renderFullStatus gained a "Files touched" section during Phase 4 acceptance
payload.over: express (dependency law); any static/UI route (scope law); debouncing appends
payload.because: the localhost-JSON-only law and locked dependency set forbid them; the latency budget is generous and consumers coalesce; an endpoint that dies on one torn log is useless

payload.chose: BD30: `harness event append` — the convention-dialect surface: --type/--payload required, --session/--source/--actor flags, optional [slug] positional (BD16); NOT best-effort (BD22 exemption) — any failure exits 1 with BD17 typed-error JSON on stderr and appends NOTHING; success prints {ok, event_id}; all writes route through appendAndProject (validate → append → regenerate); a dialect session_ended satisfies the Stop hook identically to the MCP path
payload.over: a top-level `harness append`; stdin payloads
payload.because: an explicit caller deserves real errors; BD4 fixed the name and grouping under `event` keeps the machine surface in one place; one-line flag invocations are what convention-following agents compose reliably

payload.chose: BD31: AGENTS.md protocol block — init installs AGENTS_PROTOCOL_BLOCK with the SAME marker discipline as CLAUDE.md (same markers, create-if-missing, append-if-unmarked, never touched once markers exist); carries the three BD19 clauses but a CLI-only loop (orient via `harness status`, write via `harness event append`, write-back MANDATORY) plus prohibitions: never hand-edit projections, never edit events.jsonl, corrections are new correction events; SPEC §CLI init line extended
payload.over: reusing PROTOCOL_BLOCK verbatim; different marker names per file
payload.because: AGENTS.md readers (OpenCode, Codex, plain shells) cannot be assumed to have MCP or a blocking Stop hook — the MANDATORY clause is the compensating control; the MCP loop names harness_* tools an MCP-less tool cannot call; one discipline keeps hand-edits surviving in both

payload.chose: BD32: OpenCode adapter = docs + simulated checklist, no plugin code — docs/opencode-adapter.md maps the hook surface to OpenCode plugin hooks (tool.execute.after ≈ PostToolUse via shell-out) and states the gap explicitly: OpenCode has NO exit-2-blocking Stop equivalent, so write-back rests on the AGENTS.md MANDATORY clause; a future plugin is specified as thin shell-outs only (BD4); the §3 12-step manual checklist is simulated 1:1 against the BUILT CLI in acceptance.phase5.test.ts; 5.2's checkbox stays open until the manual run happens
payload.over: building the plugin now; checking the box on the simulation alone
payload.because: OpenCode's plugin API is outside the locked dependency window and the convention dialect is the contracted v1 fallback (SPEC §Acceptance Phase 5); the simulation proves checklist ACCURACY, not agent compliance — the honest record beats the checkmark

payload.chose: BD33: serve also pushes on addDir of a new initiative directory, with a bounded wait (20×50ms) for its events.jsonl before folding (then pushes whatever exists); the SPEC 500ms SLA still asserted for appends to existing logs; the new-dir test asserts the push happens, not its speed
payload.over: usePolling; widening test timeouts alone
payload.because: a dir created and populated in one burst can lose the file add event to a chokidar/fsevents race (reproduced ~1-in-5 flake); polling is constant cost for a first-push-only race; the miss was real product behavior, not test tightness

payload.chose: BD34: Protocol CLAUDE.md moved docs/ → repo root (ceremony prep, Jul 7); internal references path-fixed; SPEC §Repo layout updated
payload.over: a thin root pointer file to docs/CLAUDE.md
payload.because: Claude Code auto-loads only a root CLAUDE.md and the 5.3 cold resume "from the record alone" needs that routing; two protocol files drift — one file, one home

payload.chose: BD35: Root AGENTS.md added as a THIN ROUTER (Jul 7, after the Codex cold start failed) pointing at docs/harness.md + docs/SPEC.md and deferring all rules to CLAUDE.md (single source); field note: a session must LAUNCH inside the repo — no root file can route a blank workspace
payload.over: a full protocol copy (one appeared untracked and was reshaped)
payload.because: Codex/OpenCode-family tools auto-read AGENTS.md, not CLAUDE.md — BD34 fixed routing for Claude Code only; duplicated rules drift (the exact reason BD34 rejected pointer+copy pairs); both cold-resume dry runs proved agents follow multi-hop routing reliably

payload.chose: BD36: serve gained a bounded reconciliation safety net (Jul 7 Codex resume) — while SSE clients are connected, the server scans initiative log size/mtime every 250ms and broadcasts any changed log the watcher missed; the watcher remains the fast path; versions seeded after watcher-ready; no scanning without clients; JSON-only localhost scope and the SPEC 500ms existing-append SLA preserved
payload.over: unbounded polling; static/UI work; treating the failure as test-only
payload.because: even the BD33 addDir fallback can miss a create+populate burst on fsevents (full `npm test` reproduced it 234/235); the failure reproduced the exact product path the test protects

payload.chose: BD37: Phase 6 (hardening + distribution readiness) added post-plan on user direction ("continue the development", Jul 7) — README, installable tarball, atomic projection writes, version single-sourcing, repo.md surfacing; scope law intact (no UI/sync/team/telemetry)
payload.over: starting post-v1 roadmap items (ui, sync)
payload.because: the contents are the gap between "SPEC-complete" and "shippable"; ui/sync would be a guard-rail violation inside the window — those need the user to reopen scope explicitly

payload.chose: BD38: Projection writes are atomic — every generated file is written to a dot-prefixed `.<target>.<pid>.<random>.tmp` in the SAME directory then renameSync over the target (atomic on POSIX same-fs); write failures remove the temp; a unit test asserts no *.tmp lingers; temp names never collide and serve's basename-filtered watcher never fires on them; generator keeps plain-overwrite semantics (init idempotency never depended on it)
payload.over: write-if-changed in the generator; fsync ceremony
payload.because: serve's /state fold, a SessionStart fold, or a human tailing plan.md must never observe a half-written projection; write-if-changed is a read per file per append to optimize nothing measured; events.jsonl is truth — projections are regenerable

payload.chose: BD39: CLI version single-sourced — src/cli/index.ts imports `version` from ../../package.json (esbuild inlines the named JSON import) and passes it to program.version(); the hardcoded "0.1.0" literal is gone; asserted in the built-CLI smoke: `--version` output equals package.json's field at test time, so a bump can never drift
payload.over: reading package.json at runtime relative to the bin
payload.because: only dist/ ships inside a tarball whose package.json location differs between npm layouts — inlining at build time has no runtime path to get wrong

payload.chose: BD40: Repo memory in the SessionStart context — .harness/repo.md surfaces as a "Repo memory (.harness/repo.md)" section after goal/current, before the phase tree, with its OWN 1,500-char budget and a line-structure-preserving clip (clipBlock) + truncation pointer; missing/unreadable/empty/byte-identical-init-stub → section omitted; IO + stub check in the handler, budget/placement/rendering in templates/status.ts; the global ≤10k guard re-proven with a ~50k repo.md; SPEC §Hooks SessionStart line extended
payload.over: folding repo.md into the goal budget; rendering it in `harness status` too
payload.because: repo-scoped memory must not compete with initiative state; the uncapped CLI print is initiative-scoped — repo.md is a context-injection concern (revisit on demand)

payload.chose: BD41: Engine manifest reclassified for bundled-bin distribution — ALL packages/engine dependencies (incl. private @harness/schema, which made the old tarball uninstallable) moved to devDependencies because dist/cli.js is fully esbuild-bundled and consumers need ZERO runtime deps; "prepack": "node build.mjs"; the engine STAYS "private": true until the user green-lights publishing (npm pack works with private); guarded by test/packaging.test.ts: npm pack → global install into temp prefix → --version answers, zero deps installed, init → new → status drive in a fixture (~1s warm)
payload.over: publishing @harness/schema to make the old manifest installable; removing "private" now
payload.because: the BD7 dependency LAW is about the USED set, which is byte-identical (still imported, still bundled); publishing an internal package nobody imports at runtime is pointless; publish is a user decision, not a build gate

payload.chose: BD42: README.md at repo root — terse/factual npm front door in the docs' register (no marketing voice): what harness is, honest pre-publish install, quickstart, the three integration surfaces, the SPEC §Record layout diagram verbatim, a CLI table, doc links; the tarball README is ONE source at root — engine "prepack" copies it in, "postpack" deletes it, .gitignore excludes it so it can never drift; packaging E2E asserts the installed package carries it and the engine dir is clean after pack
payload.over: a hand-maintained packages/engine/README.md; symlinking
payload.because: two front doors drift (the BD34/BD35 no-pointer+copy rule, kept by generating the copy at pack time only); npm pack follows symlinks inconsistently across platforms

payload.chose: BD43: Adopt-by-id sessions — harness_start_session gained an OPTIONAL session_id arg and the BD20 newest-open adoption heuristic is REMOVED ENTIRELY; session_id naming an OPEN session → adopt exactly it; an ENDED session → typed invalid_input with zero appends; UNKNOWN id → session_started appended with that id (registers it); omitted → fresh ulid, NEVER adopts; the SessionStart context now opens with "Session: <id> — pass this as session_id" (id clipped to 120 chars; ≤10k cap untouched); init's CLAUDE.md protocol START step updated; SPEC §MCP tools + §Hooks updated; all newest-open tests converted + new branch coverage
payload.over: keeping newest-open as a fallback when session_id is omitted
payload.because: the heuristic cross-adopted: a second agent's start_session silently took over the first agent's open session, so parallel sessions corrupted each other's attribution and Stop gates — the fallback IS the bug; an omitting agent gets its own identity and unwritten hook sessions surface via the 7.2 derived resume

payload.chose: BD44: Derived resume fallback — fold aggregates per-session activity from mechanical events by envelope.session: activity? = {files[] deduped first-touch order, commands count, task_changes[] "<id> → <status>"} capped at 20 + "+N more" sentinel; attached ONLY when ≥1 such event, ONLY to registered sessions, NEVER session "cli"; corrections void activity; closed_reason recorded when session_closed set ended; renderStatus renders a derived resume block for the newest summary-less session with activity unless a written-back session is newer; sessions/<id>.md gained an Activity section; describeActivity in templates/shared.ts; state shape in engine fold.ts (not payload schema); SPEC §State updated
payload.over: aggregating into unregistered stub sessions; surfacing the derived line in renderFullStatus/`harness status`
payload.because: a resume point for a session nobody registered is noise (same rule as BD21); the design scoped surfaces to the status context block + sessions/<id>.md — the CLI print already lists files_touched (revisit on demand)

payload.chose: BD45: `harness uninit [--purge]` — surgical inverse of init: unlink exactly the four shims (dirs removed only when THIS run emptied them); settings.json pruned by shim-path substring across ALL event keys (wrapped/moved commands still match), with emptied groups → arrays → hooks key pruned in order; .mcp.json loses mcpServers.harness; CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md lose the marker block + exactly one blank-line seam (missing end marker → warn, file untouched); unparseable JSON aborts exit 1; .harness/ KEPT by default ("use --purge to delete"), --purge rm -rfs it naming `harness export` as backup; managed-file deletion ONLY under --purge and only when THIS run emptied the file (resolves the byte-clean round-trip conflict, smallest deviation)
payload.over: an install manifest tracking init provenance; preserving arbitrary user JSON formatting; keeping emptied managed files even under --purge
payload.because: a manifest is a second source of truth about what is installed; init already normalizes merged files to stable JSON so stable-formatted files round-trip byte-exact; "never delete managed files" cannot coexist with the contracted byte-clean fresh-repo init → uninit --purge round-trip

payload.chose: BD46: `harness adopt <legacy-file> [slug] [--mark]` — the CLI mechanizes environment checks + marking and prints THE MIGRATION BRIEF; it never parses the legacy markdown; validation order with BD17 typed errors (file exists, .harness/ exists naming `harness init`, initiative resolves with a "create it with `harness new`" hint); the brief is self-contained and verbatim-executable: five dialect templates sharing ONE `migration-<ulid>` session id, a Step 0 session_started {"tool":"migration"} registration (added during 8.3 — without it the session_ended folds into a stub session and every later status warns), all --actor human (transcription is human-authored truth, BD26 precedent); then the repo-knowledge move into .harness/repo.md, the protocol-retirement checklist, and the verification line; --mark prepends an idempotent superseded banner between <!-- harness:superseded --> markers; paths render repo-relative; SPEC §CLI gained the adopt line

p='README.md'; s=open(p).read()
addition = '''

## Build-tool hygiene

The record (`.sofar/`) is prose committed into your repo — plans, decisions,
notes. Tools that scan your whole tree for content can ingest it. Known case:
**Tailwind v4** auto-scans every non-gitignored file for class candidates and
can mint invalid CSS from code-like prose in projections. Exclude the record
in your `globals.css`:

```css
@source not "../.sofar";
```

The same principle applies to any scanner with tree-wide globs: point it away
from `.sofar/`.
'''
s = s.rstrip() + addition
open(p,'w').write(s)
print('README updated')
EOF

payload.chose: BD53: the `.sofar` exclusion path is resolved RELATIVE TO THE STYLESHEET (Tailwind's @source contract) — dot-anchored (`.sofar`→`./.sofar`), forward-slashed — and `doctor --fix` inserts `@source not "<rel>/.sofar";` on the line AFTER the `@import "tailwindcss"` statement; idempotency rides a semantic coverage check (parse every `@source not`, strip glob tails, resolve, match `.sofar` or an ancestor)
